summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #691 in fogodev/ars-ui by fixing controlled value-change payload handling and stale timer effects in the navigation menu component, then get CI green.
summary.outcome: All fixes were applied to navigation_menu/mod.rs, tests and clippy passed, and the commit was pushed. CI progressed through formatting, snapshots, clippy, and workspace checks successfully; unit tests were running but unusually slow at session end.

repo: ars-ui uses cargo llvm-cov for coverage, cargo +nightly fmt for formatting, and a spec_conformance integration test suite alongside lib unit tests.
SetItems in the navigation menu must treat an empty registry as removal of any active rendered item and clear the open value/state.
Delay effects (OpenDelay/CloseDelay) must be cancelled when item-registry sync or controlled-value sync occurs to avoid stale timer races.
The shared value-change effect should carry the requested next value directly rather than re-reading the controlled bindable after the fact.

code.finding: ValueChange/pending_value logic and OpenDelay/CloseDelay timer handling live in this range; controlled value sync and item-registry sync interact here.
SetItems handling and open-value clearing on empty registry patched here.
Stale delay-effect cancellation and value-change payload fixes applied in this range.

workflow: Codex is triggered via `@codex review` comment on the PR and acknowledges with an eyes reaction before posting findings.
GitHub GraphQL API is used to poll review threads and check resolution status alongside `gh pr checks` for CI status.
Polling CI with sleep loops (30s, 180s, 300s intervals) is the primary strategy for tracking long-running jobs; unusually long unit test runs are left to finish rather than cancelled.
After resolving a Codex thread, a fresh `@codex review` trigger restarts the review cycle on the new head commit.

friction: Unit Tests job ran unusually long (started 20:03:03Z) with no failure output available while in progress, causing extended polling uncertainty.
Multiple 180–300 second sleep-poll cycles were needed to track CI progress, making the feedback loop slow.
Several sequential `apply_patch` calls were needed to address the Codex findings, suggesting the fixes were non-trivial to scope correctly from static analysis alone.
open_items: Unit Tests job completion and final CI green status were not confirmed within the session — needs verification that the full suite passed on commit d800edf2.
Codex returned four findings that were patched together; no confirmation that Codex reviewed the new push and found no further issues.
PR #691 merge readiness (approvals, final Codex sign-off) was not confirmed.
